code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-SEP-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with marked proteinuria and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, bilirubin present), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating possible hypoglycemia or malnutrition. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
